title: Tokyo Street Photography Simulator
description: The user operates an antique camera on a tripod overlooking the animated Littlest Tokyo scene. They must compose and capture photographs by adjusting focal length, exposure time, and camera angle, then develop the shots in a virtual darkroom panel to score points based on composition quality.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a street photography simulation. Load LittlestTokyo.glb as the main city scene centered at origin and play its built-in AnimationMixer animations. Load AntiqueCamera.glb and place it on an elevated platform (y=2, z=15) facing the city. Add a warm directional light simulating golden-hour sun (color #FFD4A0, intensity 2, position 10,15,10) with shadows enabled, plus soft ambient light (intensity 0.4). Use a perspective camera starting behind the antique camera model looking over it toward the city. Implement these interaction steps with an HTML overlay panel: Step 1 - Pan Control: left/right arrow keys rotate the antique camera model and the view camera together on Y-axis (clamp -45 to 45 degrees), display current angle. Step 2 - Tilt Control: up/down arrow keys tilt on X-axis (clamp -15 to 30 degrees), display tilt value. Step 3 - Zoom/Focal Length: mouse wheel adjusts the view camera FOV between 20 and 90, displayed as focal length (mm = 2000/FOV). Step 4 - Exposure Setting: a slider (0.5s to 4s) controls exposure time, which applies a brightness post-effect (multiply renderer toneMappingExposure between 0.3 and 2.5). Step 5 - Capture: pressing Space triggers a shutter animation (screen flashes white for 200ms, plays a CSS shutter-closing animation on a black overlay). Step 6 - Develop: after capture, a thumbnail appears in a darkroom sidebar panel with sepia CSS filter. The panel shows a composition score (0-100) calculated from how centered the Tokyo scene is in view (based on FOV and rotation values, optimal near angle=0, tilt=10, FOV=45). Step 7 - Gallery: up to 5 captures stored in the sidebar, each showing score. Validate all parameters are within range before allowing capture. Show a status bar with current pan, tilt, focal length, and exposure values. Add OrbitControls disabled by default but togglable with a 'Free Look' button that detaches from the antique camera. Use GLTFLoader and renderer with toneMapping set to ACESFilmicToneMapping.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  cameraYaw: number,         // current Y-axis rotation of antique camera in degrees, range -45 to 45
  cameraTilt: number,         // current X-axis tilt of antique camera in degrees, range -15 to 30
  focalLength: number,         // current focal length in mm, derived from FOV (2000/FOV), range ~22 to 100
  exposureTime: number,         // exposure duration in seconds, range 0.5 to 4.0
  photosCaptured: number,         // total count of photographs taken
  photosProcessed: number,         // count of photos developed in darkroom
  currentScore: number,         // accumulated points from composition quality scoring
  isDarkroomOpen: boolean,         // whether the darkroom panel is currently visible
  isCapturing: boolean,         // whether a photo capture is in progress
  tokyoAnimationTime: number,         // current playback time of LittlestTokyo animation in seconds
}
